title: What is a computer?
deck: A plain explanation of what makes something a “computer,” from phones to servers — without assuming you already speak engineer.

title: Software vs hardware
deck: Hardware is the body; software is the instructions. Here is how they depend on each other.

title: What is the internet?
deck: Not the same as “Wi‑Fi,” not the same as a website — the internet is a global network of networks.

title: How does the Internet work?
deck: Packets, routing, and protocols — a step-by-step mental model without drowning in acronyms.

title: What is a web browser?
deck: What Chrome, Firefox, Safari, and Edge actually do when you type an address.

title: What is cloud computing?
deck: Someone else’s computers, rented by the minute, accessed over the internet — and why companies use it.

title: Everyday digital security
deck: Passwords, updates, phishing, backups — high leverage habits that cost little.

title: Careers in IT (big map)
deck: Support, infrastructure, security, data, product, and software — many doors, shared curiosity.

title: How to start a business from zero
deck: A sober sequence: clarity, legal basics, offer, customers, money — without pretending it is easy.

title: How to validate a business idea
deck: Tests cheaper than quitting your job: interviews, pre-sales, and small pilots.

title: How to maintain a small business
deck: Rhythm beats heroics: cash, customers, quality, and your own bandwidth.

title: Cash flow in plain English
deck: Why “profitable” businesses still go broke — timing of money in and out.

title: Marketing basics for small business
deck: Who it is for, what you say, where you show up — before you spend on ads.

title: Sole prop, LLC, corporation — what changes?
deck: Very simplified; always confirm with a professional for your jurisdiction.

title: What is an economy?
deck: How societies decide what to make, who gets it, and what “growth” usually means in the news.

title: Supply and demand (the core picture)
deck: Why prices move when either side of the market shifts.

title: Money and inflation
deck: What money does in an economy and why prices sometimes rise broadly.

title: GDP and growth (simple)
deck: What GDP measures, what it misses, and why journalists love the word “recession.”

title: What governments do in the economy
deck: Taxes, spending, regulation, and money — high-level, non-partisan framing.

title: International trade basics
deck: Why countries trade, who gains, who hurts in the short run.

title: How to improve your brain (the boring, true version)
deck: Sleep, movement, challenge, connection — what research tends to agree on.

title: How to be more disciplined without hating yourself
deck: Systems, environment, identity, and recovery after slips.

title: How to be kind (without becoming a doormat)
deck: Respect for others and yourself; empathy with boundaries.

title: How reading works in your head
deck: Eyes, working memory, comprehension vs recognition.

title: Memory and learning that lasts
deck: Forgetting curves, active recall, spacing, sleep.

title: Decisions and planning
deck: Urgent vs important, concrete plans, buffers.

title: Attention in a noisy world
deck: Task-switch costs, deep vs shallow work, breaks.
